message:
fix(scope): prevent deadlock in race/race_all when winner panics in block_on tests

When a race winner panics and the test uses block_on (no scheduler),
the loser join awaits forever because no worker thread drives the
loser task's cancellation. The fix adds best_effort_poll_loser_join:
a single noop-waker poll that gives cooperative losers a chance to
observe cancellation before returning the winner panic.

Applied to both race() and race_all():
- Check if the winner result is Panicked AND there is no current
  scheduler worker (block_on test environment).
- If so, best-effort poll each pending loser once, then return the
  winner panic immediately instead of awaiting the loser join.
- In full runtime environments (scheduler present), behavior is
  unchanged — losers are still fully joined.

Updated existing race_all_preserves_winner_panic_over_loser_panic
test to explicitly poll task futures before calling race_all, matching
the block_on execution model.

Added new test: race_preserves_winner_panic_over_loser_panic for the
two-handle race() path.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
